paper_title: Automated Code Discovery and Evaluation for Github Repository
expected_outputs: Ranked list of recommended GitHub repositories based on user query and weighted metrics
Static code analysis reports evaluating maintainability, readability, complexity, coding standards compliance, bugs, code smells, and security vulnerabilities
Dynamic performance testing results including runtime efficiency and memory utilization
Repository ranking and evaluation visualization across repositories (Fig. 2)
extraction_notes: No GitHub repository URL for the project was provided in the source material.
No requirements.txt or environment.yml contents were provided.
No explicit dataset download URLs were provided; the system appears to retrieve repository metadata and code dynamically from the GitHub API.
The paper mentions use of the GitHub API endpoint `search/repositories`, but does not provide a full download URL.
No main entry-point script or executable filename was specified.

The methodology references tools/frameworks including GitHub API, CodeBERT, spaCy, SonarQube, Pylint, ESLint, and possible UI frameworks such as React, Angular, or Flask, but exact implementation details are not given.
Dynamic testing mentions execution against test cases and example data, but no concrete test datasets or URLs are specified.
